era_context: Continuation of the Late Bronze Age. Egypt under Thutmose III remained the dominant regional power; the Hittite Empire was consolidating its position in Anatolia; Mycenaean Greece continued its palace-period prosperity; and the Shang Dynasty maintained its control over China. This was an era of relatively stable international relations punctuated by military campaigns and trade.

events.title: Thutmose III's continued military campaigns in the Levant
events.description: Thutmose III continued his systematic campaigns to maintain Egyptian control over Levantine territories and suppress Mitanni influence. These campaigns were part of his broader strategy to consolidate the Egyptian empire and secure trade routes. The year -1398 falls within his period of active military engagement in the region.

sources.name: Karnak Temple inscriptions documenting Thutmose III's campaigns

events.certainty_note: Thutmose III's campaigns are well-documented in general, but specific events in -1398 are not individually attested.

events.title: Mycenaean trade networks in the Eastern Mediterranean
events.description: Mycenaean merchants and traders were actively engaged in commerce throughout the Eastern Mediterranean, exchanging Greek pottery, olive oil, and wine for tin, cedar, and other luxury goods. This trade network connected Mycenaean Greece with Egypt, the Levant, and Anatolia, facilitating cultural exchange and economic interdependence.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence of Mycenaean pottery in Eastern Mediterranean sites
Linear B tablets documenting trade goods

events.certainty_note: Mycenaean trade networks are well-attested archaeologically, though specific transactions in -1398 are not documented.

events.title: Shang Dynasty oracle bone divination and ritual practices
events.description: The Shang Dynasty continued its sophisticated system of oracle bone divination, which served both religious and administrative functions. These divinations addressed questions of warfare, agriculture, weather, and royal succession, reflecting the integration of religious practice with political governance.
events.certainty_note: Shang divination practices are well-attested, but specific divinations from -1398 are not individually documented.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the broader chronological framework for this period remains contested, particularly regarding the synchronization of Egyptian and Near Eastern chronologies.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1398 is similarly sparse as -1399, relying primarily on Egyptian administrative records and archaeological evidence. The absence of year-specific documentation means that this year is understood primarily through the broader context of the Late Bronze Age rather than through documented events.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Thutmose III's reign and campaigns
Mycenaean trade networks connected with contemporary Greek civilization
